transcript: Hello, I'm the writer-director of the short film Dark Circle. If you haven't watched it, would love it if you spare 11 minutes to watch it. Link is in the description. And come back here if you haven't understood the story or the ending. Several viewers recently told me that they were intrigued by the film but confused by the ending. First of all, the story is told non-linearly, which means the scenes are not told in the order in which the events happen which could be a source of confusion. There are dates shown of course at the top which should help to some extent piece together the story in chronological order. As a side note all the dates are new moon or dark moon or amavasya dates which is symbolic. In the film we go back and forth between 2010 in India and 2015 in the United States. Dark circle is really about a very disturbed couple Agni and Rumi. During a math class in 2010 as a professor passionately explains geometric properties of points and circles this couple with their dark and complicated minds draws a connection between these properties and their own lives. Which is that the only way to bring harmony and peace in their tumultuous relationship is by having mirror image twins join their drum circle. They believe that this would not only complete their circle, but would also bring much needed harmony to their relationship. They never found peace, and ultimately, in April 2015, they die by murder suicide, presumably out of extreme depression. Much of the film is about two days in the month of May, about a month after their death, when Mirror Image twins Maya and Chaya visit the house where Agni and Bhumi's spirits are presumably still residing. Even through the death, they continue to seek peace and harmony. They've finally found the mirror image twins they had been waiting for for many years, which provides closure to their troubled spirits. The film ends when these twins have this realization that Agni and Bhumi are actually dead. We see Chhaya playing drums with the mirror image copy of Agni and Bhoomi outside of the house. Maya is in total shock but still alive. It's left to the viewer's imagination whether at that point Chhaya is alive or dead. In other words, can spirits and humans actually coexist or would the twins have to cross over to Agni and Bhoomi's world in order to form this so-called dark drum circle? Thank you for watching and if you have questions please post them as comments below.
